# Hyderabad Kingsmen

> Independent coverage of the Hyderabad Kingsmen — the Hyderabad, Sindh-based franchise that debuted in the Pakistan Super League (PSL) in 2026 and reached the final in their first season. Original analysis, player profiles, and venue guides, in English and Urdu.

## Sections

- [News & Analysis](https://hyderabadkingsmen.com/posts/)
- [Player profiles](https://hyderabadkingsmen.com/players/)
- [Venue guides](https://hyderabadkingsmen.com/venues/)
- [Head-to-head](https://hyderabadkingsmen.com/head-to-head/)
- [About](https://hyderabadkingsmen.com/about/)

## About the team

The Hyderabad Kingsmen are a Pakistan Super League (PSL) franchise based in Hyderabad, Sindh, Pakistan. The team was established in 2026 when the PSL expanded from six to eight franchises. The franchise rights were acquired by the FKS consortium for PKR 1.75 billion per year at a Pakistan Cricket Board auction.

The team plays its home matches at Niaz Stadium, Hyderabad. The team is coached by Jason Gillespie and captained by Marnus Labuschagne. The team colours are black and gold, with a warrior-themed logo featuring a golden crown.

In their debut PSL 2026 season, the Kingsmen lost their first four league matches before winning their first PSL game against Karachi Kings, finishing fourth in the league stage with 10 points. They beat Multan Sultans by eigh
